LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DENNIS KERN, DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS AND PHIL GINSBURG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION CONTAINS VARIOUS ALLEGATIONS OF DISCRIMINATION, HARASSMENT, AND RETALIATION AGAINST BOTH INDIVIDUAL AND ENTITY DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO PLEAD CAUSES OF ACTION THAT ARE COGNIZABLE AGAINST INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS SEPARATE FROM CLAIMS THAT ARE NOT COGNIZABLE AGAINST ENTITY DEFENDANTS. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 52.1 TO ALLEGE EXEMPTION ON GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DENNIS KERN, DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS GINSBURG, PHIL RECREATION AND PARKS DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HEARING SET FOR SEP-11-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DENNIS KERN, DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS, PHIL GINSBURG, RECREATION AND PARKS DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. FROM THE FACE OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION CLAIMS A VIOLATION OF CIV. CODE 52.1(B) THEREFORE THE EXEMPTIONS SET FORTH UNDER GOV. CODE 905 ARE INAPPLICABLE. THE COURT OF APPEALS HAS STATED: "IN GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 905, THE LEGISLATURE ENUMERATED 12 TYPES OF CLAIMS AGAINST LOCAL PUBLIC ENTITIES THAT ARE EXCEPTED FROM THE MANDATORY FILING REQUIREMENT (GOV.CODE, 905, SUBDS (A)-(L )); CLAIMS FOR DAMAGES FOR VIOLATION OF [CAL. CIV. CODE] SECTIONS 51 AND 52.1 ARE NOT AMONG THEM." GATTO V. COUNTY OF SONOMA (2002) 98 CAL.APP.4TH 744, 763. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO FILE A GOVERNMENT CLAIM AS REQUIRED BY THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT, THEREFORE, IS FATAL TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AND LEAVE TO AMEND CANNOT CURE THE DEFECT. THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO CORRECT THE COURT'S PREVIOUS ORDER ON THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS THAT IS NOT PROPERLY BROUGHT BEFORE THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E. A. KARNOW, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR# 12353. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED AT THE HEARING THAT SHE IS SOCIAL FRIENDS WITH DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY ELIZABETH SALVESON. JUDGE MILLER PRACTICED LAW IN THE SAME FIRM AS MS SALVESON FROM DECEMBER 1981 - MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS MATTER SHE SHE DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD REASONABLY HAVE A DOUBT THAT I WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PLAINTIFF DAVID DINSLAGE'S MOTION TO TAX AND/OR STRIKE CLAIMED COSTS SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO - THE MOTION TO TAX AND/OR STRIKE CLAIMED COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DENIED AS TO THE FILING FEES BECAUSE A PUBLIC ENTITY IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER FEES INCURRED, EVEN IF NOT PAID. (GUILLEMIN V. STEIN (2002) 104 CAL.APP.4TH 156, 164.) DENIED AS TO THE PROCESS SERVER'S FEE FOR SERVING THE SUBPOENA TO DFEH BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WITHDREW HIS REQUEST TO TAX THIS COST IN HIS REPLY BRIEF. (PLAINTIFF'S REPLY BRIEF, P. 3.) GRANTED AS TO THE MESSENGER FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $205.00. DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE USE OF THE MESSENGER SERVICE WAS "REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE LITIGATION RATHER THAN MERELY CONVENIENT OR BENEFICIAL TO ITS PREPARATION." (BENACH V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (2007) 149 CAL.APP.4TH 836, 857.). COST IN THE AMOUNT OF $90.00 IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)
